decisions.value: Legs now 3x/week (Mon, Wed, Fri) instead of 1x. Upper/Lower split adopted: Mon=LegsA, Tue=Pull, Wed=LegsB, Thu=Push, Fri=LegsC
Quads designated weakest muscle group. 25 quad sets programmed across Legs A and Legs C
No RDLs, deadlifts from floor, hyperextensions, or loaded good mornings. Hip hinge via cable pull-through only
90-degree max ROM on all squat patterns (leg press, hack squat, goblet squat, Bulgarian split squat)
Metabolic Primer (AMRAP or Tabata) mandatory at start of every gym session

constraints_added: knees: Occasional knee pain reported during discovery. 90-degree ROM cap implemented on all squat patterns

schedule_changes.value: Upper/Lower split with 3 legs days (Mon, Wed, Fri)

plan_rationale_deltas: Baseline appointment — no prior WROC data. Conservative 300-400 kcal deficit initiated with EA floor protection
gymSport days raised to 2320 kcal — EA dropped below 30 kcal/kg FFM with dual sessions (~625 kcal EEE). Metabolic health protection prioritized over aggressive deficit
Leg training expanded from 1 to 3 days — athlete is female with quad weakness. 25 quad sets programmed to address weakest link
Split shifted to Upper/Lower format to accommodate 3 legs days without compromising upper body development
notes: Baseline appointment. Carol reported occasional knee pain and lower back discomfort on loaded exercises. Iron deficiency noted in health conditions — iron+Vit C pairing enforced, calcium-iron separation maintained. Energy levels mid-to-low, sleep 6-7hrs quality 6-7/10. Athlete satisfied with current food preferences — no changes requested.
